Introduction
§ 01Who We Are

Forhemit is a California Public Benefit Corporation that helps business owners transition their companies to employee ownership through ESOPs. We serve as a stewardship partner from first conversation through closing and beyond, managing the process so owners can focus on running their business.

Our role is to bring structure, clarity, and trusted advisors to every ESOP transaction. We work with owners who want to preserve their company’s legacy, take care of their employees, and achieve a fair outcome—without the disruption of a private equity sale.

§ 02Why ESOPs

An ESOP offers business owners something a traditional sale cannot: the ability to sell on their terms, at fair market value, while preserving the company’s culture and rewarding the employees who helped build it.

A.Tax advantages. Sellers who reinvest in Qualified Replacement Securities can defer 100% of federal capital gains tax under IRC §1042. The company becomes permanently income-tax-exempt.
B.Employee retention. Employees receive retirement benefits through the ESOP trust, creating alignment and reducing turnover.
C.Legacy preservation. The company remains independent. No outside buyer dictates strategy, culture, or staffing.
D.Fair market value. An independent appraiser sets the price. Sellers don’t leave money on the table.
§ 03What We’re Looking For

We partner with brokers who have clients that match our program. Ideal candidates are:

A.Owner-operated. The founder or a small group of owners holds a controlling interest and is ready to step back.
B.Profitable. At least $2M in annual revenue with consistent positive EBITDA. The business can service acquisition debt.
C.Stable workforce. A team of 15+ W-2 employees with reasonable tenure. High turnover complicates ESOP feasibility.
D.Clear transition motivation. Retirement, burnout, health, or a desire to reward employees. Not a distressed sale.
§ 04What’s In It for the Broker

ESOPs expand the set of outcomes you can offer your seller clients. When a PE sale or strategic acquisition isn’t the right fit, an ESOP may be.

A.Referral fees. We pay referral fees on closed transactions. Standard terms are disclosed during our introductory call.
B.Differentiated offering. Few brokers can offer ESOP as an exit path. This positions you as a full-spectrum advisor.
C.Commission protection. Your existing commission arrangement is preserved. We don’t compete for the listing.
D.Done-for-you process. We manage the entire ESOP transaction. Your involvement is limited to the introduction and coordination you’re already doing.
§ 05Next Steps

If you have a client who may be a fit, or you’d like to learn more about how ESOPs work as an exit strategy, let’s schedule a brief call.

15-minute call: We’ll walk through the program, answer your questions, and discuss any candidates in your pipeline. No obligation.
